title: PractitionerRole · provider enrichment (specialty + care_site)
notes:
Branch dimension: how the PractitionerRole__provider edge ENRICHES an existing cdm_ours_fhir.provider row that was first INSERTed by Practitioner__provider. Two edges target the same OMOP table 'provider'; the orchestrator runs them in PLAN order — Practitioner__provider (mode 'truncate', the INSERT) BEFORE PractitionerRole__provider (mode 'update', the UPDATE).

*** RUNNER ISOLATION LIMIT — why only the baseline variant is asserted here ***
PractitionerRole__provider is the ONLY mode:'update' edge in the entire PLAN (script/etl-plan.ts). Its SQL (mapspec/etl/PractitionerRole__provider.sql) is an `UPDATE cdm_ours_fhir.provider p ... FROM staging.practitionerrole_provider v WHERE p.provider_id = referenceToId(v.practitioner_ref)`. The case runner (script/run-cases.ts) isolates a case by rewriting only `staging.` → `t_staging_<suf>.` (the `subStaging` helper) and by wrapping INSERT-mode bodies with `INSERT INTO t_cdm_<suf>.<table>`. For mode:'update' it runs the body VERBATIM (run-cases.ts line ~138: `p.mode === 'update' ? body : ...`). There is NO `cdm_ours_fhir.` → `t_cdm_<suf>.` substitution. So the UPDATE's target table stays the hardcoded production `cdm_ours_fhir.provider`, NOT the isolated `t_cdm_<suf>.provider` that the INSERT wrote and that the assertion reads. Result: the enrichment lands in the wrong (shared) schema and the isolated row the runner asserts is never touched. This is a structural limitation of the runner for update-mode edges, not a fixable case error — confirmed by reproducing the INSERT+UPDATE against scratch dbg_* schemas: with the UPDATE's `cdm_ours_fhir.provider` left unrewritten, dbg_cdm.provider.specialty_concept_id stayed 38004446 / care_site_id stayed NULL even though staging.practitionerrole_provider correctly held (practitioner_ref=prac-1, org_ref=org-1, specialty_code=62247001).

Four enrichment variants were authored, hand-verified against the live DB, and then DROPPED because the runner cannot isolate them (it would need to redirect the UPDATE to t_cdm). Their TRUE expected behavior (what the full pipeline produces) is documented below for the record; only the baseline (no PractitionerRole, INSERT-only) is kept as a green assertion.

WHAT THE INSERT (mapspec/etl/Practitioner__provider.sql) SETS for the baseline row:
  provider_id                 = hashtextextended(Practitioner.id,0)  (= referenceToId('prac-1'); surrogate PK, NOT asserted)
  provider_name               = coalesce(family,'') || ', ' || coalesce(given,'')   → 'Ward668, Olympia319'
  npi                         = Practitioner.identifier[system=us-npi].value         → '9999928192'
  specialty_concept_id        = 38004446  (HARDCODED 'General Practice', copied from ETL-Synthea)
  care_site_id                = NULL
  gender_concept_id           = male→8507 / female→8532 / other→8521 / unknown→8551 / else 0
  provider_source_value       = Practitioner.id UUID (= 'prac-1' here)
  specialty_source_value      = NULL
  specialty_source_concept_id = 38004446
  gender_source_value         = Practitioner.gender (raw)
  gender_source_concept_id    = male→8507 / female→8532 / else 0

WHAT THE UPDATE WOULD CHANGE (the dropped variants), if isolatable — and ONLY these two columns:
  specialty_concept_id = COALESCE(std.concept_id, p.specialty_concept_id)
  care_site_id         = COALESCE(referenceToId(v.org_ref), p.care_site_id)
Everything else (provider_name, npi, specialty_source_value, specialty_source_concept_id, gender_*) is LEFT UNTOUCHED — so specialty_source_concept_id stays 38004446 and specialty_source_value stays NULL even when specialty_concept_id moves.

Dropped variant (a) — PractitionerRole w/ SNOMED specialty 62247001 + Organization/org-1: specialty_concept_id → 38003851 'Family Medicine Physician' (SNOMED 62247001 'Maps to' standard, Provider domain, verified in vocab.concept), care_site_id → referenceToId('org-1') = -3583712261691553566. specialty_source_concept_id stays 38004446.
Dropped variant (b) — PractitionerRole w/ Organization but NO specialty: specialty_concept_id stays 38004446 (COALESCE keeps inserted), care_site_id → referenceToId('org-1').
Dropped variant (c) — PractitionerRole w/ SNOMED specialty but NO Organization: specialty_concept_id → 38003851, care_site_id stays NULL (org_ref NULL → COALESCE keeps inserted NULL).
Dropped variant (e) — real-Synthea shape: specialty coded in NUCC provider-taxonomy (208D00000X), NOT SNOMED. The stage-1 view (mapspec/views/PractitionerRole__provider.view.json) extracts specialty_code ONLY from system=http://snomed.info/sct: `specialty.first().coding.where(system=%snomedSystem).code.first()`, so a NUCC code yields specialty_code NULL → std.concept_id NULL → specialty_concept_id stays 38004446; Organization still sets care_site_id. This matches the live cohort: all 261 cdm_ours_fhir.provider rows keep specialty_concept_id=38004446 and care_site_id=NULL because Synthea PractitionerRole.specialty is always NUCC.

VERIFIED concept_ids (vocab.concept, May 2026 bundle): 38004446 'General Practice'; SNOMED 62247001 'Family medicine specialist' 'Maps to' standard 38003851 'Family Medicine Physician'; 8507 MALE; 8532 FEMALE. referenceToId('org-1') = -3583712261691553566, referenceToId('prac-1') = 5693730440195230850.

BASELINE (the asserted variant): a Practitioner with NO PractitionerRole. The runner filters edges by present FHIR resource types (run-cases.ts: `PLAN.filter(p => present.has(tbl(p.src)))`), so with no fhir.practitioner_role the PractitionerRole edge is skipped entirely and only the INSERT runs — making this variant fully isolatable and exercising the unenriched provider row that PractitionerRole would later augment. The only target table is provider; no other OMOP table is produced.

cases.desc: (baseline) Practitioner only, NO PractitionerRole → INSERT-only provider row, no enrichment: specialty_concept_id 38004446 (General Practice default), care_site_id NULL, specialty_source_concept_id 38004446, gender male→8507. (Enrichment variants a/b/c/e dropped — runner cannot isolate the mode:'update' edge, which hardcodes UPDATE cdm_ours_fhir.provider; see notes.)
